Home.quote: The body adapts to what you ask of it.

PhysioPage.title: Scientific rigour, human attention.
PhysioPage.lead: I’m a physiotherapist, trained in Belgium, with a passion for movement, health and performance. My path has taken me through varied settings: from professional sport to general private practice, and specialised fields such as senology and geriatrics.
PhysioPage.parcoursP1: These experiences let me discover different approaches to physiotherapy and develop a rich, nuanced view of care. I learned there is no single way to treat - but a multitude of paths, to adapt to each patient.
PhysioPage.parcoursP2: My practice draws on scientifically validated techniques while giving listening an essential place. Every person is unique, with their own story, goals and needs. My role is to understand that uniqueness and offer tailored support.
PhysioPage.parcoursP3: Driven by a sincere desire to help, I commit to guiding my patients towards their best version - in recovery, prevention or performance.
PhysioPage.trailP1: A trail runner myself, I know the demands of effort and recovery from the inside - an understanding of sport that informs every session.
PhysioPage.formationsIntro: My path has been guided by a constant drive to gain recognised skills from leading institutions, so I can offer care grounded in best practice.

formationsList.body: Physiotherapy degree obtained in Brussels, then Swiss equivalence validated by the Swiss Red Cross - the authorisation to practise in Switzerland.
Taught by the official trainers of the Leduc School, an internationally recognised reference method.

formationsList.org: Dry Needling · David G. Simons Academy
formationsList.body: With an internationally renowned institution specialised in the treatment of myofascial pain.
A long-form training in biomechanics, manual therapy and athlete care, from amateur to elite level.
Regular training days on LPG technologies: endermologie, scar treatment and drainage.

PhysioPage.formationsOutro: Today I keep expanding my skills across my three areas of expertise - general physio, sports physio and dermato-functional physio - through seminars and exchanges with other health professionals.

Prestations.title: Three areas of expertise, one tailored care.
Prestations.lead: Medical (with or without a prescription), sport and aesthetic - individualised and recognised by Swiss basic insurance.

medical.title: General & medical physio.
medical.lead: Any musculoskeletal condition or injury, recognised by Swiss basic insurance.
medical.card: Recognised by all Swiss basic insurance. Any musculoskeletal condition or injury.
medical.body: On prescription or direct access, precise care for musculoskeletal conditions: orthopaedics, traumatology, functional rehabilitation and acute or chronic pain.
medical.metaDescription: Medical and general physiotherapy in Lausanne: orthopaedics, traumatology, rehabilitation, dry needling. With or without a prescription, recognised by basic insurance.

sport.title: Sports physio.
sport.lead: From amateur to elite athlete: assessment, planning and recovery, from a physiotherapist who is himself a trail runner.
sport.card: Personalised sports follow-up, assessment and goal planning. For every level, from getting back in shape to competition.
sport.body: A first 30-minute session to assess your needs, then personalised follow-up. For athletes and amateurs alike: goal planning, coaching and recovery. A trail runner myself, I understand from the inside the demands of effort and recovery.
sport.metaDescription: Sports physiotherapy in Lausanne: assessment, goal planning, prevention and recovery. Athlete follow-up from amateur to elite level.

max.title: Alongside Maximilien Drion.
max.text: I support <link>Maximilien Drion</link>, a ski-mountaineering athlete, as a physiotherapist and osteopath in his preparation for international and Olympic competitions.
max.text2: A demanding follow-up - injury prevention, recovery and planning - as close as possible to the constraints of elite sport.

esthetic.title: Aesthetic treatments.
esthetic.lead: Lymphatic drainage, endermologie and scar work - with or without a prescription. LPG®-trained and equipped.
esthetic.card: Lymphatic drainage, endermologie and scar work, with or without a prescription. LPG®-trained and equipped.
esthetic.body: Lymphatic drainage, multilayer bandaging, scar work and LPG® sessions - with or without a prescription, for visible, lasting results.
esthetic.metaDescription: Aesthetic treatments in Lausanne: lymphatic drainage, LPG® endermologie, scar work. With or without a prescription.
esthetic.machinesIntro: The practice is equipped with the latest LPG® technologies, used alongside manual techniques for visible, lasting results.

machines.name: Cellu M6 Infinity®
machines.body: The reference in medical and aesthetic endermologie. Its motorised treatment head gently mobilises the tissue: it boosts circulation, drains the tissue and reactivates the natural production of collagen and elastin - painless and non-invasive.

machines.name: Huber 360® Evolution
machines.body: A motorised platform that engages the entire musculoskeletal system: balance, deep strengthening, mobility and coordination. Its built-in functional assessment tracks progress session after session.

CabinetPage.lead: A space designed to make you feel cared for, inside Maison-B, within Dr Burgener’s practice.

equipments.name: Iyashi Dôme
equipments.text: Japanese long-infrared technology to support detoxification.

CabinetPage.accessText: Maison-B (Dr Burgener’s practice), 400 m from Lausanne station. A few spaces on the street, Alpha-Palmiers car park 200 m away.

booking.lead: A question? Get in touch about any of my services. Book your session online through OneDoc - I usually reply within 1 to 2 working days.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This policy describes how data is processed on this site, in line with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nLPD).
privacy.dataText: No health data is collected through this site. The contact details you share (email, phone) are used only to answer your request.
privacy.cookiesText: With your consent, Google Analytics 4 measures site audience (Consent Mode v2, anonymised IP). You can decline or withdraw your consent at any time via the cookie banner.
privacy.mapsText: The practice map (Google Maps) is only loaded after you accept cookies.
privacy.rightsText: You have the right to access, correct and delete your data. For any request, contact Thomas Derême.
privacy.note: This document should be reviewed by a professional before going live.

items.a: No. Thomas sees patients with or without a prescription. With a prescription, physiotherapy is recognised by Swiss basic insurance; without one, sessions last 30 or 60 minutes.
Yes - medical physiotherapy on prescription is recognised by Swiss basic insurance (LAMal).
At 12 Rue du Midi, 1003 Lausanne, in Dr Burgener’s Maison-B, 400 m from Lausanne station.

items.q: Do you offer dry needling and lymphatic drainage?
items.a: Yes. Thomas offers dry needling, lymphatic drainage, LPG® endermologie and scar work, among others.
Online via OneDoc, or by phone at +41 76 688 72 81.
